summary: Comprehensive testing of Telegram Intelligence Platform completed. Backend APIs 100% success rate (31/31 tests passed). Frontend fully functional with entities overview, channel details, sparkline charts, filtering, and navigation working correctly.

ui_bugs.issue: Console warnings about chart width/height being -1 during initial render. Charts still render correctly after layout settles.
Close button (X icon) has visibility issue when trying to click programmatically
ui_bugs.workaround: Clicking backdrop or using force=True works

ui_bugs.issue: Error boundary triggered when opening compare modal - component error in CompareColumn
ui_bugs.impact: Compare modal may not render correctly

issues: React hydration warnings about <span> inside <tbody> and <tr> - likely from development tooling, not affecting production

passed_tests: All filter parameters (search, type, activity, members range, growth range, red flags, sort)
Channel overview with full data structure (/api/telegram-intel/channel/{username}/overview)
Channel profile, topCards, metrics, timeline, recentPosts, relatedChannels
Frontend: Entities page loads with table
Frontend: Sparkline trend charts render in table rows
Frontend: Filter drawer opens with all presets (Fast Growing, High Reach, Low Risk, New Emerging)
Frontend: All filter fields present (Type, Activity, Members, Reach, Growth, Red Flags, Sort)
Frontend: Navigation to channel detail page
Frontend: Channel detail page with all cards (header, activity, audience, product, AI summary, snapshot, health, related)
Frontend: Performance Timeline chart renders with time range buttons
Frontend: Back navigation to entities page
action_items: Fix CompareColumn component error when opening compare modal
Consider adding minWidth/minHeight to Sparkline container to prevent console warnings
Review React hydration warnings (likely dev tooling related)
critical_code_review_comments: Sparkline component should handle edge case when container has no dimensions yet
CompareColumn component needs error handling for missing data fields
seed_data_creation: Seeded 20 mock channels via /api/telegram-intel/admin/seed endpoint for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend APIs fully functional with 31 tests passing. Frontend working with minor console warnings. MTProto client installed but not authorized (requires TG_API_ID from user). All channel data is MOCK data - not connected to live Telegram API. Compare modal has a component error that needs fixing.
mocked_data_note: ALL CHANNEL DATA IS MOCK - Generated from mock data generator. MTProto client is installed but not authorized - requires manual auth step with TG_API_ID, TG_API_HASH, and phone number.
